aiTitle: Design mock Linear API integration test suite

input.plan:
# Add architecture visuals to integration-test design doc + render to HTML

## Context

`docs/integration-test-architecture.md` exists and is the design doc for the Linear initiative ([project](https://linear.app/ababushkin/project/test-drain-cycle-failure-modes-and-resume-without-a-real-linear-cycle-910b954af13f)). It's about to go to a team / tech lead / manager for review-and-approval.

Two design choices in the doc carry most of the review risk and are hardest to convey in prose:

1. **The seam choice** — real `claude -p` + mocked Linear, glued by an MCP shim that translates child MCP calls into GraphQL against the same in-memory store the parent hits over HTTP. The §Pillar 2 paragraph "Why an MCP shim and not just `LINEAR_API_URL` in the child env" takes ~200 words to explain something a system-context diagram explains in one glance.
2. **The seam closes end-to-end** — that an end-to-end happy-path drain actually flows through the mock both at the parent boundary and through the child's MCP. A sequence diagram is the cheapest proof.

A third visual — the **failure-mode coverage matrix** — turns KR 2 ("every failure path covered") from a sentence into something a reviewer can scan and push back on ("what about X?").

After the three visuals land, render the whole doc to HTML via `/render-html` so the reviewer experience matches a product OKR review rather than a GitHub markdown blob.

The user explicitly chose architecture-focused visuals; cost-split, slice-dependency graph, and per-scenario-contract card are deferred — sequencing/slice mechanics are already clear from the prose.

## Approach

Three edits to `docs/integration-test-architecture.md` and one render. Sections are added; no existing sections are rewritten.

### Edit 1 — System context diagram (new H2 "System context")

Placement: between §Context and §Workflow.

Heading: `## System context` — matches P14's detector (`/system( context)?|architecture/i`), so render-html wraps the inline SVG in `.p-diagram` with the caption styled.

Inline SVG content (authored in the markdown as a ```svg fenced block):

- **Nodes** (~7):
  - `IntegrationHarness` (pytest fixture)
  - `orchestrator.run()` (parent, real)
  - `claude -p` (child, real, spawned per issue)
  - `MockLinear` (FastAPI, mocked)
  - `linear_mcp_shim` (stdio MCP, mocked)
  - `in-memory store` (Cycle / Issue / Label / WorkflowState + call log)
- **Boundary**: a dashed group around `MockLinear` + `linear_mcp_shim` + `in-memory store` labelled "MOCKED". Everything else is real.
- **Edges**:
  - Harness → MockLinear: `populate scenario`
  - Harness → orchestrator: `run(repos, limits)`
  - orchestrator → MockLinear: `GraphQL: current_cycle, pending_issues, set_state, get_issue, resolve_state` (solid)
  - orchestrator → claude -p: `subprocess.spawn` (solid)
  - claude -p → linear_mcp_shim: `MCP stdio: save_issue, save_comment, get_issue` (solid, accent colour)
  - linear_mcp_shim → MockLinear: `GraphQL POST` (solid)
  - both `MockLinear` and `linear_mcp_shim` write into `in-memory store` (thin lines)

Caption: "Solid = control flow. Dashed boundary = mocked. Real `claude -p` is the only path that exercises the production MCP-tool surface; the shim is the smallest seam that lets us mock Linear without forking the official MCP."

### Edit 2 — Happy-path sequence diagram (new H3 inside §Pillar 2)

Placement: at the end of §Pillar 2 (`### Pillar 2 — linear_mcp_shim …`), where the "Why an MCP shim" prose paragraph currently sits. The sequence diagram is the visual answer to that paragraph; the paragraph stays as the textual answer above it.

Heading: `### Happy-path sequence` — placed inside an existing P14-matching parent doesn't matter; P14 also detects on `/sequence/i` at H2/H3 level (P14 detector is per-section, not depth-restricted).

Inline SVG content (authored as a ```svg fenced block; ~8–10 messages, vertical lifelines):

- **Lifelines** (5, left to right): `Harness`, `orchestrator`, `MockLinear`, `claude -p`, `linear_mcp_shim`
- **Messages** (top to bottom):
  1. Harness → MockLinear: `populate(Cycle, Issue, Label)`
  2. Harness → orchestrator: `run(repos, limits)`
  3. orchestrator → MockLinear: `pending_issues` → `[Issue]`
  4. orchestrator → MockLinear: `set_state(Todo → In-Progress)`
  5. orchestrator → claude -p: `spawn`
  6. claude -p → linear_mcp_shim: `get_issue(id)` (dashed = MCP)
  7. linear_mcp_shim → MockLinear: `GraphQL get_issue`
  8. claude -p → linear_mcp_shim: `save_issue(Done)` + `save_comment(...)`
  9. linear_mcp_shim → MockLinear: `GraphQL set_state(Done)`, `comment_create`
  10. orchestrator → MockLinear: `get_issue` (verify Done) → orchestrator returns `Result`

Caption: "End-to-end happy path. The parent (left) hits `MockLinear` directly over HTTP; the child (right) hits it indirectly via `linear_mcp_shim` over stdio MCP. Both write into the same in-memory store, so scenarios assert on one unified call log."

### Edit 3 — Failure-mode coverage matrix (new H2 "Failure-mode coverage")

Placement: at the top of §Scenario catalogue, before the existing groupings (Best case / Per-issue halts / etc.). The catalogue then reads as filling in the matrix.

Heading: `## Failure-mode coverage`

Body: an introductory sentence ("The matrix below names every halt path in the production code and the scenario that covers it. KR 2's halt-coverage guard fails the suite when a new halt slug lands without a matching row.") then a markdown table:

| Halt path | Source | Scenario | Spawns claude? | Slice |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Missing `repo:` label | `orchestrator.py` repo-resolve | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_missing_repo_label` | no | 7 |
| `repo:` label → nonexistent path | `orchestrator.py` repo-resolve | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_unknown_repo_path` | no | 7 |
| Worktree `add` fails | `orchestrator.py` worktree | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_dirty_worktree` | no | 7 |
| `set_state` (Todo→In-Progress) HTTP 500 | `linear.py` set_state | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_set_state_500` | no | 6 |
| Child exits without setting Done | `orchestrator._drain_one_issue` | `test_real_claude_halts::test_not_done_exit` | yes (`--max-turns 1`) | 8 |
| Child exits non-zero | `orchestrator._drain_one_issue` | `test_real_claude_halts::test_child_nonzero` | yes | 8 |
| Per-issue token cap exceeded | `worker.py` breach loop | `test_breach::test_token_cap` | yes (verbose prompt) | 9 |
| Per-issue time cap exceeded | `worker.py` breach loop | `test_breach::test_time_cap` | yes (`wait 60s` prompt) | 9 |
| Revert `set_state` (In-Progress→Todo) fails after halt | `orchestrator.py` revert | `test_real_claude_halts::test_revert_failure` | either | 8 |
| `DependencyCycleError` from `pending_issues` | `linear.py` pending_issues | `test_cycle_halts::test_dep_cycle` | no | 10 |
| Cycle-cap breach after N Done | `worker.py` cycle accumulator | `test_cycle_halts::test_cycle_cap` | yes | 10 |
| Linear 429 / malformed JSON / slow-beyond-timeout | `linear._post` | `test_linear_edges::*` | no | 12 |

The table is intentionally plain markdown — render-html's shell styles tables cleanly out of the box (no pattern needed). Cells with `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__*` tool names use inline code formatting.

Footnote under the table: "Source paths are illustrative — the halt-coverage guard (`test_halt_coverage`) reads them from `mark_error` slugs in `drain_cycle/**.py` at suite time. A new halt slug with no matching row fails the suite."

### Edit 4 — Render to HTML

Run `/render-html docs/integration-test-architecture.md`. The skill writes `docs/integration-test-architecture.html` next to the source, self-contained, with:

- P14 fires on §System context → SVG diagram wrapped in `.p-diagram` with caption.
- P14 fires on §Pillar 2's `### Happy-path sequence` H3 → second SVG wrapped likewise. (P14 detects per-section, not just H2 — confirmed in `html-patterns.md`.)
- §Failure-mode coverage table → shell-default `<table>` styling.
- TOC sidebar includes "System context", "Failure-mode coverage" as new top-level H2 entries.

Open the file in the browser, verify TOC navigation works and both SVGs render. Skill workflow already requires the user-facing handoff sentence ("Open the file in your browser to verify TOC navigation and that no console errors appear.").

## Critical files

To modify:
- `docs/integration-test-architecture.md` — three new sections (System context, Happy-path sequence inside §Pillar 2, Failure-mode coverage at top of §Scenario catalogue). No existing prose is rewritten; the additions are insertions.

To create (by `/render-html`, not by hand):
- `docs/integration-test-architecture.html` — self-contained HTML render.

## Authoring notes for the SVGs

- Use `viewBox="0 0 860 340"` and the node/edge classes from P14 (`p-node`, `p-edge`, `p-edge-async`, `p-node-emphasis`). Don't introduce custom hex colours — colours come from the shell's `:root` palette (`--accent`, `--ok`, `--muted`, etc.). Verified via `references/html-patterns.md` P14.
- Cap each diagram at ~7 nodes / ~10 edges per P14's authoring guidance. Both diagrams hit that.
- Edge labels ≤ 24 chars; longer text goes in the caption.
- Each `<svg>` carries `role="img"` and a short `aria-label`; the caption beneath is the long description.

## Verification

End-to-end:
- `cat docs/integration-test-architecture.md | grep -c '```svg'` returns `2` — both SVG blocks land.
- `cat docs/integration-test-architecture.md | grep -c '^## '` increases by 2 (System context + Failure-mode coverage).
- `/render-html docs/integration-test-architecture.md` runs without warnings.
- `docs/integration-test-architecture.html` exists; size under 500KB (P14's hard cap from the skill).
- Open in browser:
  - Network panel: zero requests after load (self-containment rule).
  - Both SVGs render visibly with arrows + labels.
  - TOC sidebar lists the two new H2s.
  - Failure-mode-coverage table is legible at desktop and narrow widths.
- Original markdown is still review-readable (no skill-only structure that breaks on GitHub).

## Approved Plan (edited by user):
# Add architecture visuals to integration-test design doc + render to HTML

## Context

`docs/integration-test-architecture.md` exists and is the design doc for the Linear initiative ([project](https://linear.app/ababushkin/project/test-drain-cycle-failure-modes-and-resume-without-a-real-linear-cycle-910b954af13f)). It's about to go to a team / tech lead / manager for review-and-approval.

Two design choices in the doc carry most of the review risk and are hardest to convey in prose:

1. **The seam choice** — real `claude -p` + mocked Linear, glued by an MCP shim that translates child MCP calls into GraphQL against the same in-memory store the parent hits over HTTP. The §Pillar 2 paragraph "Why an MCP shim and not just `LINEAR_API_URL` in the child env" takes ~200 words to explain something a system-context diagram explains in one glance.
2. **The seam closes end-to-end** — that an end-to-end happy-path drain actually flows through the mock both at the parent boundary and through the child's MCP. A sequence diagram is the cheapest proof.

A third visual — the **failure-mode coverage matrix** — turns KR 2 ("every failure path covered") from a sentence into something a reviewer can scan and push back on ("what about X?").

After the three visuals land, render the whole doc to HTML via `/render-html` so the reviewer experience matches a product OKR review rather than a GitHub markdown blob.

The user explicitly chose architecture-focused visuals; cost-split, slice-dependency graph, and per-scenario-contract card are deferred — sequencing/slice mechanics are already clear from the prose.

## Approach

Three edits to `docs/integration-test-architecture.md` and one render. Sections are added; no existing sections are rewritten.

### Edit 1 — System context diagram (new H2 "System context")

Placement: between §Context and §Workflow.

Heading: `## System context` — matches P14's detector (`/system( context)?|architecture/i`), so render-html wraps the inline SVG in `.p-diagram` with the caption styled.

Inline SVG content (authored in the markdown as a ```svg fenced block):

- **Nodes** (~7):
  - `IntegrationHarness` (pytest fixture)
  - `orchestrator.run()` (parent, real)
  - `claude -p` (child, real, spawned per issue)
  - `MockLinear` (FastAPI, mocked)
  - `linear_mcp_shim` (stdio MCP, mocked)
  - `in-memory store` (Cycle / Issue / Label / WorkflowState + call log)
- **Boundary**: a dashed group around `MockLinear` + `linear_mcp_shim` + `in-memory store` labelled "MOCKED". Everything else is real.
- **Edges**:
  - Harness → MockLinear: `populate scenario`
  - Harness → orchestrator: `run(repos, limits)`
  - orchestrator → MockLinear: `GraphQL: current_cycle, pending_issues, set_state, get_issue, resolve_state` (solid)
  - orchestrator → claude -p: `subprocess.spawn` (solid)
  - claude -p → linear_mcp_shim: `MCP stdio: save_issue, save_comment, get_issue` (solid, accent colour)
  - linear_mcp_shim → MockLinear: `GraphQL POST` (solid)
  - both `MockLinear` and `linear_mcp_shim` write into `in-memory store` (thin lines)

Caption: "Solid = control flow. Dashed boundary = mocked. Real `claude -p` is the only path that exercises the production MCP-tool surface; the shim is the smallest seam that lets us mock Linear without forking the official MCP."

### Edit 2 — Happy-path sequence diagram (new H3 inside §Pillar 2)

Placement: at the end of §Pillar 2 (`### Pillar 2 — linear_mcp_shim …`), where the "Why an MCP shim" prose paragraph currently sits. The sequence diagram is the visual answer to that paragraph; the paragraph stays as the textual answer above it.

Heading: `### Happy-path sequence` — placed inside an existing P14-matching parent doesn't matter; P14 also detects on `/sequence/i` at H2/H3 level (P14 detector is per-section, not depth-restricted).

Inline SVG content (authored as a ```svg fenced block; ~8–10 messages, vertical lifelines):

- **Lifelines** (5, left to right): `Harness`, `orchestrator`, `MockLinear`, `claude -p`, `linear_mcp_shim`
- **Messages** (top to bottom):
  1. Harness → MockLinear: `populate(Cycle, Issue, Label)`
  2. Harness → orchestrator: `run(repos, limits)`
  3. orchestrator → MockLinear: `pending_issues` → `[Issue]`
  4. orchestrator → MockLinear: `set_state(Todo → In-Progress)`
  5. orchestrator → claude -p: `spawn`
  6. claude -p → linear_mcp_shim: `get_issue(id)` (dashed = MCP)
  7. linear_mcp_shim → MockLinear: `GraphQL get_issue`
  8. claude -p → linear_mcp_shim: `save_issue(Done)` + `save_comment(...)`
  9. linear_mcp_shim → MockLinear: `GraphQL set_state(Done)`, `comment_create`
  10. orchestrator → MockLinear: `get_issue` (verify Done) → orchestrator returns `Result`

Caption: "End-to-end happy path. The parent (left) hits `MockLinear` directly over HTTP; the child (right) hits it indirectly via `linear_mcp_shim` over stdio MCP. Both write into the same in-memory store, so scenarios assert on one unified call log."

### Edit 3 — Failure-mode coverage matrix (new H2 "Failure-mode coverage")

Placement: at the top of §Scenario catalogue, before the existing groupings (Best case / Per-issue halts / etc.). The catalogue then reads as filling in the matrix.

Heading: `## Failure-mode coverage`

Body: an introductory sentence ("The matrix below names every halt path in the production code and the scenario that covers it. KR 2's halt-coverage guard fails the suite when a new halt slug lands without a matching row.") then a markdown table:

| Halt path | Source | Scenario | Spawns claude? | Slice |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Missing `repo:` label | `orchestrator.py` repo-resolve | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_missing_repo_label` | no | 7 |
| `repo:` label → nonexistent path | `orchestrator.py` repo-resolve | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_unknown_repo_path` | no | 7 |
| Worktree `add` fails | `orchestrator.py` worktree | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_dirty_worktree` | no | 7 |
| `set_state` (Todo→In-Progress) HTTP 500 | `linear.py` set_state | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_set_state_500` | no | 6 |
| Child exits without setting Done | `orchestrator._drain_one_issue` | `test_real_claude_halts::test_not_done_exit` | yes (`--max-turns 1`) | 8 |
| Child exits non-zero | `orchestrator._drain_one_issue` | `test_real_claude_halts::test_child_nonzero` | yes | 8 |
| Per-issue token cap exceeded | `worker.py` breach loop | `test_breach::test_token_cap` | yes (verbose prompt) | 9 |
| Per-issue time cap exceeded | `worker.py` breach loop | `test_breach::test_time_cap` | yes (`wait 60s` prompt) | 9 |
| Revert `set_state` (In-Progress→Todo) fails after halt | `orchestrator.py` revert | `test_real_claude_halts::test_revert_failure` | either | 8 |
| `DependencyCycleError` from `pending_issues` | `linear.py` pending_issues | `test_cycle_halts::test_dep_cycle` | no | 10 |
| Cycle-cap breach after N Done | `worker.py` cycle accumulator | `test_cycle_halts::test_cycle_cap` | yes | 10 |
| Linear 429 / malformed JSON / slow-beyond-timeout | `linear._post` | `test_linear_edges::*` | no | 12 |

The table is intentionally plain markdown — render-html's shell styles tables cleanly out of the box (no pattern needed). Cells with `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__*` tool names use inline code formatting.

Footnote under the table: "Source paths are illustrative — the halt-coverage guard (`test_halt_coverage`) reads them from `mark_error` slugs in `drain_cycle/**.py` at suite time. A new halt slug with no matching row fails the suite."

### Edit 4 — Render to HTML

Run `/render-html docs/integration-test-architecture.md`. The skill writes `docs/integration-test-architecture.html` next to the source, self-contained, with:

- P14 fires on §System context → SVG diagram wrapped in `.p-diagram` with caption.
- P14 fires on §Pillar 2's `### Happy-path sequence` H3 → second SVG wrapped likewise. (P14 detects per-section, not just H2 — confirmed in `html-patterns.md`.)
- §Failure-mode coverage table → shell-default `<table>` styling.
- TOC sidebar includes "System context", "Failure-mode coverage" as new top-level H2 entries.

Open the file in the browser, verify TOC navigation works and both SVGs render. Skill workflow already requires the user-facing handoff sentence ("Open the file in your browser to verify TOC navigation and that no console errors appear.").

## Critical files

To modify:
- `docs/integration-test-architecture.md` — three new sections (System context, Happy-path sequence inside §Pillar 2, Failure-mode coverage at top of §Scenario catalogue). No existing prose is rewritten; the additions are insertions.

To create (by `/render-html`, not by hand):
- `docs/integration-test-architecture.html` — self-contained HTML render.

## Authoring notes for the SVGs

- Use `viewBox="0 0 860 340"` and the node/edge classes from P14 (`p-node`, `p-edge`, `p-edge-async`, `p-node-emphasis`). Don't introduce custom hex colours — colours come from the shell's `:root` palette (`--accent`, `--ok`, `--muted`, etc.). Verified via `references/html-patterns.md` P14.
- Cap each diagram at ~7 nodes / ~10 edges per P14's authoring guidance. Both diagrams hit that.
- Edge labels ≤ 24 chars; longer text goes in the caption.
- Each `<svg>` carries `role="img"` and a short `aria-label`; the caption beneath is the long description.

## Verification

End-to-end:
- `cat docs/integration-test-architecture.md | grep -c '```svg'` returns `2` — both SVG blocks land.
- `cat docs/integration-test-architecture.md | grep -c '^## '` increases by 2 (System context + Failure-mode coverage).
- `/render-html docs/integration-test-architecture.md` runs without warnings.
- `docs/integration-test-architecture.html` exists; size under 500KB (P14's hard cap from the skill).
- Open in browser:
  - Network panel: zero requests after load (self-containment rule).
  - Both SVGs render visibly with arrows + labels.
  - TOC sidebar lists the two new H2s.
  - Failure-mode-coverage table is legible at desktop and narrow widths.
- Original markdown is still review-readable (no skill-only structure that breaks on GitHub).

No production code changes. No tests added. No commits — the doc + HTML are local artefacts the user reviews before deciding to commit.

aiTitle: Design mock Linear API integration test suite

content.text: Edit 2 — Happy-path sequence diagram at end of §Pillar 2.

input.old_string:
**Why an MCP shim and not just `LINEAR_API_URL` in the child env.** MCP is a separate protocol from raw GraphQL; the official Linear MCP server speaks MCP over stdio to claude and HTTPS to Linear. Setting `LINEAR_API_URL` in the child has no effect — there's no way to redirect the official server without forking it. A purpose-built shim is the smallest seam.

### How scenarios steer claude
input.new_string:
**Why an MCP shim and not just `LINEAR_API_URL` in the child env.** MCP is a separate protocol from raw GraphQL; the official Linear MCP server speaks MCP over stdio to claude and HTTPS to Linear. Setting `LINEAR_API_URL` in the child has no effect — there's no way to redirect the official server without forking it. A purpose-built shim is the smallest seam.

#### Happy-path sequence

The seam closes end-to-end. One issue, picked up and driven to Done:

```svg
<svg viewBox="0 0 860 500" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Sequence diagram of a single-issue happy-path drain showing messages between Harness, orchestrator, MockLinear, claude-p, and linear_mcp_shim.">
  <defs>
    <marker id="seq-arr" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M0,0 L10,5 L0,10z" fill="currentColor"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="seq-arr-accent" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M0,0 L10,5 L0,10z" fill="var(--accent)"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <g class="p-node">
    <rect x="20" y="20" width="120" height="44" rx="8"/>
    <text x="80" y="47" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-title">Harness</text>
  </g>
  <g class="p-node">
    <rect x="180" y="20" width="140" height="44" rx="8"/>
    <text x="250" y="47" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-title">orchestrator</text>
  </g>
  <g class="p-node">
    <rect x="360" y="20" width="140" height="44" rx="8"/>
    <text x="430" y="47" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-title">MockLinear</text>
  </g>
  <g class="p-node">
    <rect x="540" y="20" width="120" height="44" rx="8"/>
    <text x="600" y="47" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-title">claude -p</text>
  </g>
  <g class="p-node">
    <rect x="700" y="20" width="140" height="44" rx="8"/>
    <text x="770" y="47" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-title">linear_mcp_shim</text>
  </g>

  <line x1="80"  y1="64" x2="80"  y2="475" stroke="var(--rule)" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4 4"/>
  <line x1="250" y1="64" x2="250" y2="475" stroke="var(--rule)" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4 4"/>
  <line x1="430" y1="64" x2="430" y2="475" stroke="var(--rule)" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4 4"/>
  <line x1="600" y1="64" x2="600" y2="475" stroke="var(--rule)" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4 4"/>
  <line x1="770" y1="64" x2="770" y2="475" stroke="var(--rule)" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4 4"/>

  <path d="M80 95 L430 95" marker-end="url(#seq-arr)" class="p-edge"/>
  <text x="255" y="88" text-anchor="middle" class="p-edge-label">populate(Cycle, Issue)</text>

  <path d="M80 130 L250 130" marker-end="url(#seq-arr)" class="p-edge"/>
  <text x="165" y="123" text-anchor="middle" class="p-edge-label">run(repos, limits)</text>

  <path d="M250 170 L430 170" marker-end="url(#seq-arr)" class="p-edge"/>
  <text x="340" y="163" text-anchor="middle" class="p-edge-label">pending_issues</text>
  <path d="M430 195 L250 195" marker-end="url(#seq-arr)" class="p-edge" stroke-dasharray="4 4"/>
  <text x="340" y="210" text-anchor="middle" class="p-edge-label">[Issue]</text>

  <path d="M250 240 L430 240" marker-end="url(#seq-arr)" class="p-edge"/>
  <text x="340" y="233" text-anchor="middle" class="p-edge-label">set_state(In-Progress)</text>

  <path d="M250 280 L600 280" marker-end="url(#seq-arr)" class="p-edge"/>
  <text x="425" y="273" text-anchor="middle" class="p-edge-label">spawn</text>

  <path d="M600 320 L770 320" marker-end="url(#seq-arr-accent)" class="p-edge-async"/>
  <text x="685" y="313" text-anchor="middle" class="p-edge-label">get_issue (MCP)</text>
  <path d="M770 345 L430 345" marker-end="url(#seq-arr)" class="p-edge"/>
  <text x="600" y="338" text-anchor="middle" class="p-edge-label">GraphQL get_issue</text>

  <path d="M600 385 L770 385" marker-end="url(#seq-arr-accent)" class="p-edge-async"/>
  <text x="685" y="378" text-anchor="middle" class="p-edge-label">save_issue(Done) · save_comment</text>
  <path d="M770 410 L430 410" marker-end="url(#seq-arr)" class="p-edge"/>
  <text x="600" y="403" text-anchor="middle" class="p-edge-label">GraphQL set_state(Done)</text>

  <path d="M250 455 L430 455" marker-end="url(#seq-arr)" class="p-edge"/>
  <text x="340" y="448" text-anchor="middle" class="p-edge-label">get_issue (verify Done)</text>
</svg>
```

The parent (left half) talks to `MockLinear` directly over HTTP. The child (right half) reaches the same store indirectly, via `linear_mcp_shim` over MCP stdio. Both write into the in-memory store, so the test's call log records the full conversation across both surfaces — the parent's `pending_issues` / `set_state` calls and the child's `save_issue` / `save_comment` calls interleave in one ordered list that scenarios assert against.

### How scenarios steer claude

+The parent (left half) talks to `MockLinear` directly over HTTP. The child (right half) reaches the same store indirectly, via `linear_mcp_shim` over MCP stdio. Both write into the in-memory store, so the test's call log records the full conversation across both surfaces — the parent's `pending_issues` / `set_state` calls and the child's `save_issue` / `save_comment` calls interleave in one ordered list that scenarios assert against.
### How scenarios steer claude
Scenarios drive child behaviour through three knobs the harness sets per-scenario:

## Failure-mode coverage

The matrix below names every halt path in the production code and the scenario that covers it. KR 2's halt-coverage guard fails the suite when a new halt slug lands without a matching row.

| Halt path | Source | Scenario | Spawns claude? | Slice |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Missing `repo:` label | `orchestrator.py` repo-resolve | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_missing_repo_label` | no | 7 |
| `repo:` label → nonexistent path | `orchestrator.py` repo-resolve | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_unknown_repo_path` | no | 7 |
| Worktree `add` fails | `orchestrator.py` worktree | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_dirty_worktree` | no | 7 |
| `set_state` (Todo→In-Progress) HTTP 500 | `linear.py` set_state | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_set_state_500` | no | 6 |
| Child exits without setting Done | `orchestrator._drain_one_issue` | `test_real_claude_halts::test_not_done_exit` | yes (`--max-turns 1`) | 8 |
| Child exits non-zero | `orchestrator._drain_one_issue` | `test_real_claude_halts::test_child_nonzero` | yes | 8 |
| Per-issue token cap exceeded | `worker.py` breach loop | `test_breach::test_token_cap` | yes (verbose prompt) | 9 |
| Per-issue time cap exceeded | `worker.py` breach loop | `test_breach::test_time_cap` | yes (`wait 60s` prompt) | 9 |
| Revert `set_state` (In-Progress→Todo) fails after halt | `orchestrator.py` revert | `test_real_claude_halts::test_revert_failure` | either | 8 |
| `DependencyCycleError` from `pending_issues` | `linear.py` pending_issues | `test_cycle_halts::test_dep_cycle` | no | 10 |
| Cycle-cap breach after N Done | `worker.py` cycle accumulator | `test_cycle_halts::test_cycle_cap` | yes | 10 |
| Linear 429 / malformed JSON / slow-beyond-timeout | `linear._post` | `test_linear_edges::*` | no | 12 |

lines: - **MCP-shim correctness** — the shim must implement the same tool schemas the official Linear MCP exposes for `save_issue` and `save_comment`, otherwise claude's tool calls land with wrong shapes. Slice 3 spike: capture the official schemas (from the Linear MCP docs or by running the real one briefly), implement matching ones in the shim, verify a single happy-path drain reaches Done end-to-end. **Kill-switch**: if the schemas drift between Linear MCP versions, pin the shim to a captured snapshot and add a periodic check.

- **Breach scenario reproducibility** — with real claude, breach scenarios depend on prompts that reliably exceed tight per-issue caps. Slice 8 spike: confirm a verbose prompt (e.g. "write a 2000-line poem") plus `per_issue_tokens=1000` reliably triggers token breach across 5 consecutive runs. **Kill-switch**: if not reliable, fall back to time-breach only (tight `per_issue_seconds` plus a "sleep 60s" prompt is robust because the breach loop polls wall-clock).

file.content: --- name: render-html description: > Convert a markdown design doc, plan, ADR, or roadmap into a single self-contained HTML file for human review. Use when an artefact is about to go to review or sign-off and the reviewer experience matters — markdown flattens spatial content (alternatives, dependency graphs, diffs, timelines) that HTML can present faithfully. Trigger phrases: "render this doc as HTML", "make this reviewable", "convert design doc to HTML", "I want to share this plan", "share this for review", "make a readable version". Use this skill whenever the user is about to hand a markdown artefact to a human reviewer, even if they don't explicitly say the word HTML. pack: engineering lifecycle_stage: review principles_implemented: - source: eng-agentic id: P7 bucket: standalone - source: eng-universal id: P1 bucket: standalone length_target: 120-180 author: Anton Babushkin predecessor: repo: none skill: none relation: new kept_from_predecessor: n/a changed_from_predecessor: n/a --- # Render HTML ## Purpose This skill converts a markdown artefact — design doc, task list, ADR, roadmap, postmortem — into one self-contained HTML file written next to the source as `.html`. The deliverable is for humans about to review or sign off on the artefact, not for archival or publication. It is the operational form of agentic Principle 7 (memory lives in artefacts): the doc itself, made navigable and legible, is the persistent record reviewers and future agents read. And it is a lever on universal Principle 1 (shipped is not done; observed is done): better review UX raises the rate at which review actually happens, which raises the rate at which outcomes get observed. ## When to use - The user asks to "render", "convert to HTML", "make this reviewable", or "share this doc" and points at a markdown file. - A markdown artefact is about to go to a human reviewer for design review, code review prep, ADR sign-off, or stakeholder alignment. - The doc is long enough that navigation matters (more than ~3 H2 sections) or contains structure that flattens badly in markdown previewers. ## When not to use - **Drafting** — write the markdown first. HTML rendering is for finished or near-finished artefacts, not works in progress. The output is regenerated each time the source changes; rendering mid-draft burns cycles for no review benefit. - **Single-screen documents** — a 30-line note doesn't earn the overhead of a TOC and shell. Send the markdown. - **Documents the reviewer will edit** — HTML round-trip back to markdown is not supported. If reviewers want to suggest edits, send the markdown or use `/crit`. - **Multi-document sites** — one source in, one file out. For a site, use a static-site generator. ## Inputs - Path to a single `.md` file with at least one H1 and structured H2/H3 sections. - Optional: prior `.html` exists alongside the source (triggers overwrite confirmation). ## Outputs - A single HTML file written to `.html` — same directory as the source, same basename, `.html` extension. - The file is self-contained: inline `

content.content:
1	# Integration test architecture
2	
3	**Linear initiative:** [Test drain-cycle failure modes and resume without a real Linear cycle](https://linear.app/ababushkin/project/test-drain-cycle-failure-modes-and-resume-without-a-real-linear-cycle-910b954af13f)
4	
5	The OKR (Goal + Key Results + appetite + kill condition) is on the Linear project. This doc is the design — what gets built, why, in what order — and is the input to `shape-planning-and-task-breakdown` when the initiative is sliced into issues.
6	
7	## Context
8	
9	Today `drain-cycle` only tests against a real Linear cycle. Anything past happy-path becomes expensive: failure modes need deliberately broken issues, resume needs an actual halt-and-restart, and usage tracking needs real Claude sessions. Unit tests cover individual modules but stop at the function boundary — they monkey-patch `linear._post` and swap `claude -p` for marker-touching shell scripts (`tests/test_orchestrator_iteration.py:22`, `tests/test_linear_pending_issues.py:65`).
10	
11	We need **scenario-level integration tests** that drive the orchestrator end-to-end against a controllable Linear surface, covering:
12	
13	- **Best-case drains** — dependency-ordered execution, runlog shape, state transitions
14	- **Per-issue failure paths** — every halt condition in `orchestrator._drain_one_issue` (repo resolution, worktree setup, `set_state` failure, breach kill, not-Done exit)
15	- **Cycle-level halts** — `DependencyCycleError`, cycle-cap breach propagation
16	- **Resume semantics** — halt mid-cycle, re-run, observe pickup of in-flight issues reverted to Todo
17	- **Session/usage tracking** — `_UsageAccumulator` totals, cycle aggregates in runlog, per-issue caps triggering breaches
18	
19	The drain has no native retry (deliberate, per `docs/design-decisions.md`); "retry" here means **resume** — a re-invocation against the same cycle.
20	
21	## System context
22	
23	The seam choice in one picture: real `claude -p`, mocked Linear, glued by an MCP shim that translates child MCP calls into GraphQL against the same in-memory store the parent hits over HTTP.
24	
25	```svg
26	<svg viewBox="0 0 860 360" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Integration test system context: real harness, orchestrator, and claude-p on the left; mocked MockLinear, linear_mcp_shim, and shared in-memory store on the right.">
27	  <defs>
28	    <marker id="arr" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto-start-reverse">
29	      <path d="M0,0 L10,5 L0,10z" fill="currentColor"/>
30	    </marker>
31	    <marker id="arr-accent" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto-start-reverse">
32	      <path d="M0,0 L10,5 L0,10z" fill="var(--accent)"/>
33	    </marker>
34	  </defs>
35	
36	  <rect x="300" y="8" width="540" height="344" rx="14" fill="none" stroke="var(--muted)" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="6 5"/>
37	  <text x="820" y="26" text-anchor="end" font="700 11px 'Inter', ui-sans-serif, sans-serif" fill="var(--muted)" letter-spacing="0.08em">MOCKED</text>
38	
39	  <g class="p-node">
40	    <rect x="40" y="30" width="200" height="60" rx="10"/>
41	    <text x="140" y="56" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-title">IntegrationHarness</text>

47	    <text x="140" y="194" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-sub">parent · real</text>

75	  <text x="160" y="125" class="p-edge-label">run()</text>
76

78	  <text x="298" y="118" class="p-edge-label">GraphQL HTTP</text>
79	
80	  <path d="M140 210 L140 270" marker-end="url(#arr)" class="p-edge"/>
81	  <text x="160" y="245" class="p-edge-label">spawn</text>
82	
83	  <path d="M240 300 L340 300" marker-end="url(#arr-accent)" class="p-edge-async"/>
84	  <text x="290" y="292" text-anchor="middle" class="p-edge-label">MCP stdio</text>
85	
86	  <path d="M440 270 L440 90" marker-end="url(#arr)" class="p-edge"/>
87	  <text x="460" y="185" class="p-edge-label">GraphQL POST</text>
88	
89	  <path d="M540 70 L620 170" marker-end="url(#arr)" class="p-edge"/>
90	  <text x="565" y="115" class="p-edge-label">writes</text>
91	
92	  <path d="M540 290 L620 195" marker-end="url(#arr)" class="p-edge"/>
93	  <text x="565" y="255" class="p-edge-label">writes</text>
94	</svg>
95	```
96	
97	Solid = control / data flow over HTTP or subprocess. Dashed (accent) = MCP stdio between the child and the shim. The dashed boundary marks what's mocked: every byte that would otherwise hit `api.linear.app` is intercepted, but `claude -p` itself is the production binary every run. Both the parent's HTTP calls and the child's MCP calls land in one shared store, so scenarios assert against one unified call log.
98	
99	## Workflow
100	
101	1. **`/shape-initiative`** — done. Linear project created with the six-field OKR (linked above).
102	2. **`shape-planning-and-task-breakdown`** — convert the *Slices* section below into Linear issues under the new initiative; assign slices 1–4 to the current cycle, 5–12 to the backlog.
103	3. **`agent-skills:test-driven-development`** during build — each slice ships a failing scenario before the supporting infra.
104	4. **`agent-skills:code-review-and-quality`** per slice before commit, per `AGENTS.md`.
105	
106	## Approach
107	
108	**Parent HTTP mock + Linear-MCP shim + real `claude -p` + in-process driver.** The spawned session is real claude every time; only the Linear surface is mocked. Three pillars.
109	
110	**Alternatives rejected** (draft for the ADR slice):
111	
112	- **Scripted/fake `claude -p`.** Cheaper, faster, deterministic. Rejected because a scripted child stops being an integration test — it cannot validate the real session's usage stream, MCP tool calls, prompt-to-completion behaviour, or production OTel spans against actual data.
113	- **Hybrid: real claude only on happy-path; scripted child for failure determinism.** Two patterns to maintain; weaker default signal; gives up real-claude coverage on usage-tracking edge cases.
114	- **Function-injection / `LinearClient` protocol on the parent.** Cheaper than HTTP but skips the HTTP boundary and adds an abstraction layer that earns nothing else. The existing module-level functions in `linear.py` drive easily against an HTTP fake once `LINEAR_API_URL` is overridable.
115	- **VCR-style recorded fixtures.** Realistic but awkward for failure injection, expensive to maintain under schema drift.
116	
117	Slice 1 produces a short ADR at `docs/adrs/0001-integration-test-architecture.md` recording the chosen approach, these rejected alternatives, and revisit conditions.
118	
119	### Cost, runtime, and determinism
120	
121	Real claude makes the suite costly, slow, and non-deterministic on numbers.
122	
123	- **Cost / runtime budget**: ~$0.05–$0.20 per spawning scenario, ~15–30 seconds per scenario. Full suite of ~30 scenarios: ~$1–$3 and ~15 minutes. Run on demand, not as part of the default suite.
124	- **Default `pytest` run** covers only the pre-spawn halt scenarios (no claude spawned) — fast and deterministic, suitable for repeated local invocation while iterating.
125	- **Determinism strategy** — split assertions:
126	  - **Exact** — exit codes, final Linear state, halt reason class, runlog file presence, structural shape of runlog JSON, set of MCP tool calls observed.
127	  - **Predicate** — `tokens > 0`, `0 < cost_usd < per_issue_cost_usd`, `num_turns >= 1`, `model is not None`. No exact values.
128	  - **Tolerant snapshot** — `dirty-equals` matches keys and types, ignores values.
129	- **Cost guard**: scenarios that spawn claude carry `@pytest.mark.real_claude`. Default `pytest` invocation excludes the marker; the `real_claude` slice opts in via `pytest -m real_claude` and runs on demand. `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` required.
130	
131	### Pillar 1 — `MockLinear`: an in-process fake GraphQL server
132	
133	A FastAPI/Starlette app on a random localhost port, started per-test via a pytest fixture. It speaks the five GraphQL operations `linear.py` uses (`current_cycle`, `pending_issues`, `get_issue`, `set_state`, `resolve_state` / WorkflowState lookup) plus an `IssueUpdate` mutation the scripted child hits to flip an issue to Done.
134	
135	State lives in a typed in-memory store: `Cycle`, `Issue`, `Label`, `WorkflowState`. The store records every call (operation, variables, timestamp) so tests can assert call sequence and counts.
136	
137	Fault injection is **per-operation, per-call-index**: `mock.fail_after(op="set_state", call=2, mode="http_500")`. Modes: `http_500`, `http_429`, `graphql_error`, `malformed_json`, `slow_response(seconds)`, `connection_reset`. The drain has no retry, so "fail" means "this single call breaks" — tests then verify the halt path.
138	
139	**Production change required**: `linear.py:18` hard-codes `https://api.linear.app/graphql`. Replace with `os.environ.get("LINEAR_API_URL", "https://api.linear.app/graphql")`. No CLI flag — env var only.
140	
141	### Pillar 2 — `linear_mcp_shim`: a Linear MCP server backed by the mock
142	
143	A small Python stdio MCP server (built on the official `mcp` SDK) that exposes the two tools the drain-cycle prompt uses today: `save_issue` (state transitions) and `save_comment`. Each tool translates its call into a GraphQL POST against `MockLinear`, sharing the same backing store as the parent's HTTP surface.
144	
145	The harness writes a custom `.mcp.json` into each test worktree registering the shim under the name `linear` (matching the existing tool prefix `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__*`). The orchestrator already symlinks `.mcp.json` from the configured source into the worktree (`orchestrator.py:257-259`); the harness arranges for the test source to be the shim-configured file.
146	
147	The shim records every tool call (tool name, arguments, timestamp) on the shared store, so scenarios assert against both parent GraphQL calls and child MCP calls through one call log.
148	
149	**Why an MCP shim and not just `LINEAR_API_URL` in the child env.** MCP is a separate protocol from raw GraphQL; the official Linear MCP server speaks MCP over stdio to claude and HTTPS to Linear. Setting `LINEAR_API_URL` in the child has no effect — there's no way to redirect the official server without forking it. A purpose-built shim is the smallest seam.
150

225	The parent (left half) talks to `MockLinear` directly over HTTP. The child (right half) reaches the same store indirectly, via `linear_mcp_shim` over MCP stdio. Both write into the in-memory store, so the test's call log records the full conversation across both surfaces — the parent's `pending_issues` / `set_state` calls and the child's `save_issue` / `save_comment` calls interleave in one ordered list that scenarios assert against.
226	
227	### How scenarios steer claude
228	
229	Scenarios drive child behaviour through three knobs the harness sets per-scenario:
230	
231	- **Issue body** — written into `MockLinear` and read by claude via `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__get_issue`. The body is the natural prompt seam (e.g. "create `<id>.txt` containing 'hello'; then mark this issue Done"). Library lives in `tests/integration/prompts/*.md`.
232	- **Per-issue limits** — `limits.yml` overrides (e.g. `per_issue_tokens=2000`, `per_issue_seconds=30`) used with verbose prompts to provoke breach scenarios deterministically.
233	- **Claude flags** — the harness may inject `--max-turns 1` for "exit without Done" cases (claude exits before completing the workflow), via the `_CLAUDE_CMD` patch point at `orchestrator.py:25`.
234	
235	The drain-cycle prompt (`drain_cycle/prompt.py:39-42`) stays unchanged — same prompt the real drain uses.
236	
237	### Pillar 3 — `IntegrationHarness`: a fixture that drives `orchestrator.run()` in-process
238	
239	A pytest fixture that:
240	
241	- Creates a temp `HOME` so `~/.drain-cycle/repos.yml`, `~/.drain-cycle/limits.yml`, and `~/.drain-cycle/runs/` redirect to a tmpdir (`monkeypatch.setenv("HOME", ...)` plus any path-resolution helpers in `cli.py` / `runlog.py`).
242	- Initialises one or more real git repos in tmpdir (existing pattern in `test_orchestrator_iteration.py`).
243	- Writes a `repos.yml` mapping label names to those repo paths.
244	- Writes a `.mcp.json` in each repo registering the Linear-MCP shim under `linear`.
245	- Starts `MockLinear` and populates it with the scenario's cycle, issues (with prompt body), and labels.
246	- Sets `LINEAR_API_URL`, `LINEAR_API_KEY=test`, `MOCK_LINEAR_URL=http://localhost:<port>`, `OTEL_SDK_DISABLED=true` env vars. Verifies `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is set for scenarios that spawn claude.
247	- Optionally appends extra flags to `_CLAUDE_CMD` (e.g. `--max-turns 1`) for scenarios that need to coerce claude's behaviour.
248	- Calls `orchestrator.run(repos, limits)` directly and returns a `Result`: exit code, runlog JSON, parent GraphQL call log, child MCP call log, final mock state.
249	
250	Tests assert against `Result`. One scenario per `test_*` function. Helpers (`make_cycle`, `make_issue`, `expect_runlog`, `expect_calls`) keep scenarios short.
251	
252	**Per-scenario contract.** Every scenario declares:
253	
254	1. **Setup** — `Cycle`, `Issue[]`, `Label[]`, `WorkflowState[]`, repos.yml mapping, limits.yml overrides, transcript fixture name(s), any fault injection.
255	2. **Expected exit code** — orchestrator return.
256	3. **Expected runlog assertions** — a list of (a) structural shape (keys, types) matched via `dirty-equals`, (b) predicate assertions on numeric fields (`tokens > 0`, `cost_usd < limits.per_issue_cost_usd`), (c) exact assertions on deterministic fields (`final_linear_state`, `halt_reason`).
257	4. **Expected final mock state** — issue states the mock should end in (e.g. `{ "ABA-1": "Done", "ABA-2": "Todo" }` after a halt on issue 2).
258	5. **Expected call sequence** — ordered `(surface, operation, predicate-on-args)` tuples across both parent GraphQL and child MCP calls. Strict by default; `expect_calls(strict=False)` for tolerant cases where claude's tool-call order varies between runs.
259	
260	A collection-time lint test fails any scenario missing (3), (4), or (5).
261	
262	## Production code changes required
263	
264	Minimal. Every test-only seam reuses existing structure.
265	
266	1. **`drain_cycle/linear.py:18`** — `_GRAPHQL_URL` reads from `LINEAR_API_URL` env var, default unchanged. One-line change.
267	2. **`drain_cycle/runlog.py`** — verify the run-log directory honours `HOME` re-resolution; slice 1 greps for `Path.home`, `expanduser`, and `HOME`. If the path caches at module load, add an accessor (e.g. `_run_log_dir()`) so the harness re-resolves after `monkeypatch.setenv("HOME", ...)`.
268	3. **`drain_cycle/orchestrator.py:25`** — `_CLAUDE_CMD` is already module-level. Tests patch it.
269	
270	No new abstraction layers, no `LinearClient` Protocol — the existing module-level functions drive easily against an HTTP fake once the URL is configurable.
271	
272	## Test-infra correctness checks (one-time pre-slice work)
273	
274	Risks the plan-review surfaced. Each lands before dependent slices.
275	
276	- **Telemetry isolation** — drain-cycle wires httpx OTel auto-instrumentation in `telemetry.py`. The harness sets `OTEL_SDK_DISABLED=true` to keep tests from exporting spans. Confirmed in slice 2.
277	- **MCP-shim correctness** — the shim must implement the same tool schemas the official Linear MCP exposes for `save_issue` and `save_comment`, otherwise claude's tool calls land with wrong shapes. Slice 3 spike: capture the official schemas (from the Linear MCP docs or by running the real one briefly), implement matching ones in the shim, verify a single happy-path drain reaches Done end-to-end. **Kill-switch**: if the schemas drift between Linear MCP versions, pin the shim to a captured snapshot and add a periodic check.
278	- **Breach scenario reproducibility** — with real claude, breach scenarios depend on prompts that reliably exceed tight per-issue caps. Slice 8 spike: confirm a verbose prompt (e.g. "write a 2000-line poem") plus `per_issue_tokens=1000` reliably triggers token breach across 5 consecutive runs. **Kill-switch**: if not reliable, fall back to time-breach only (tight `per_issue_seconds` plus a "sleep 60s" prompt is robust because the breach loop polls wall-clock).
279	
280	## Failure-mode coverage
281	
282	The matrix below names every halt path in the production code and the scenario that covers it. KR 2's halt-coverage guard fails the suite when a new halt slug lands without a matching row.
283	
284	| Halt path | Source | Scenario | Spawns claude? | Slice |
285	|---|---|---|---|---|
286	| Missing `repo:` label | `orchestrator.py` repo-resolve | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_missing_repo_label` | no | 7 |
287	| `repo:` label → nonexistent path | `orchestrator.py` repo-resolve | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_unknown_repo_path` | no | 7 |
288	| Worktree `add` fails | `orchestrator.py` worktree | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_dirty_worktree` | no | 7 |
289	| `set_state` (Todo→In-Progress) HTTP 500 | `linear.py` set_state | `test_pre_spawn_halts::test_set_state_500` | no | 6 |
290	| Child exits without setting Done | `orchestrator._drain_one_issue` | `test_real_claude_halts::test_not_done_exit` | yes (`--max-turns 1`) | 8 |
291	| Child exits non-zero | `orchestrator._drain_one_issue` | `test_real_claude_halts::test_child_nonzero` | yes | 8 |
292	| Per-issue token cap exceeded | `worker.py` breach loop | `test_breach::test_token_cap` | yes (verbose prompt) | 9 |
293	| Per-issue time cap exceeded | `worker.py` breach loop | `test_breach::test_time_cap` | yes (`wait 60s` prompt) | 9 |
294	| Revert `set_state` (In-Progress→Todo) fails after halt | `orchestrator.py` revert | `test_real_claude_halts::test_revert_failure` | either | 8 |
295	| `DependencyCycleError` from `pending_issues` | `linear.py` pending_issues | `test_cycle_halts::test_dep_cycle` | no | 10 |
296	| Cycle-cap breach after N Done | `worker.py` cycle accumulator | `test_cycle_halts::test_cycle_cap` | yes | 10 |
297	| Linear 429 / malformed JSON / slow-beyond-timeout | `linear._post` | `test_linear_edges::*` | no | 12 |
298	
299	Source paths are illustrative — the halt-coverage guard (`test_halt_coverage`) reads them from `mark_error` slugs in `drain_cycle/**.py` at suite time. A new halt slug with no matching row fails the suite loudly.
300	
301	## Scenario catalogue (initial coverage)
302	
303	Grouped by area; one test function each. Target end-state, not a single slice — slices below build the infra and add scenarios incrementally.
304	
305	Scenarios that spawn `claude -p` carry `@pytest.mark.real_claude` (excluded from the default `pytest` run; opt-in via `-m real_claude`). Scenarios that halt pre-spawn run on every default invocation.
306	
307	**Best case** *(real_claude)*
308	- 3-issue dependency-ordered drain reaches Done in order; runlog totals positive and sum across issues; child MCP calls include `save_issue` (transition to Done) and `save_comment` (summary) per issue
309	- Mixed Todo + Backlog states picked up; correct planning
310	- Single-issue drain
311	- Empty cycle exits cleanly *(no claude spawned)*
312	
313	**Per-issue halts** (`orchestrator._drain_one_issue` paths)
314	- Missing `repo:` label → halt + reason *(no claude spawned)*
315	- `repo:` label resolves to nonexistent path → halt *(no claude spawned)*
316	- Worktree `add` fails (dirty tree) → halt *(no claude spawned)*
317	- `set_state` (Todo→In-Progress) fails with HTTP 500 → halt *(no claude spawned)*
318	- Child exits without setting Done *(real_claude; coerced via `--max-turns 1`)* → halt + reason matches `final_linear_state`
319	- Child exits non-zero *(real_claude; provoked by an invalid prompt or a tool that errors)* → halt
320	- Per-issue token cap exceeded mid-stream *(real_claude; verbose prompt + `per_issue_tokens=1000`)* → breach kill; runlog records breach scope/metric/limit/observed
321	- Per-issue time cap exceeded *(real_claude; prompt "wait 60s" + `per_issue_seconds=10`)* → time breach recorded
322	- Revert `set_state` (In-Progress→Todo) fails after a halt → halt-reason concatenates *(real_claude or pre-spawn)*
323	
324	**Cycle-level halts**
325	- `DependencyCycleError` from `pending_issues` → halt before any issue attempted; `cycle_halt_reason` set; runlog otherwise empty *(no claude spawned)*
326	- After two Done issues, cycle-cap breach detected → third issue not attempted; halt reason names cumulative metric *(real_claude)*
327	
328	**Resume** *(real_claude)*
329	- Run halts on issue 2; re-run with the same cycle picks up issue 2 (mock has it back at Todo via the revert) and issue 3; second runlog file separate from first; final mock state correct
330	
331	**Session / usage tracking** *(real_claude; predicate assertions on numbers)*
332	- Single-issue drain produces `usage.cumulative.input_tokens > 0`, `usage.cumulative.output_tokens > 0`, `peak_context > 0`, `cost_usd > 0`, `num_turns >= 1`, `session_id` non-empty, `is_error == false`, `model` non-empty
333	- Multi-issue drain: cycle aggregates (`cycle_tokens_cumulative`, `cycle_cost_usd`, `cycle_duration_seconds`) equal the sum of per-issue values
334	- Worker dedup invariant (`worker.py:416`) verified indirectly: re-emit a non-deterministic prompt and confirm `usage.cumulative` is monotonically non-decreasing within a turn; explicit dedup behaviour stays unit-tested
335	
336	**Linear edge cases** *(no claude spawned — parent halts before pre-spawn `set_state` succeeds)*
337	- 500, 429, GraphQL error, malformed JSON, slow-within-timeout (drain proceeds), slow-beyond-timeout (halts)
338	
339	**Out of scope for MVP**
340	- Honeycomb/OTel span assertions (separate slice — needs a fake OTLP collector; production code wires spans cleanly per `drain_cycle/telemetry.py`)
341	- True MCP-server fake for children (scripted-child + direct GraphQL POST satisfies the contract)
342	
343	**In MVP, bounded** — slice 4 ships one subprocess smoke test that spawns the real `drain-cycle` binary against the mock and asserts exit code plus runlog presence. This single test covers `cli.py`'s secrets-load order and process exit-code path. Larger subprocess scenarios stay out of scope.
344	
345	**Note on the worker `_UsageAccumulator` dedup invariant** — the worker dedupes by `message.id` (`worker.py:416`). Real claude rarely emits duplicate ids in practice, so the dedup branch is exercised by existing unit tests, not by integration scenarios. Plan accordingly: keep the dedup unit test (`test_usage_accumulator_dedup.py` or equivalent); the integration suite asserts on monotonicity, not on dedup directly.
346	
347	## Slices (verifiable build order)
348	
349	Each slice ships in one session and ends with a passing scenario.
350	
351	1. **Production seam + ADR** — `LINEAR_API_URL` env var in `linear.py`; unit test confirms override; default unchanged. Runlog `HOME` re-resolution verified (grep + accessor if needed). **Deliverable**: `docs/adrs/0001-integration-test-architecture.md` recording the chosen approach, rejected alternatives, and revisit conditions.
352	2. **`MockLinear` skeleton** — FastAPI app with `current_cycle` and `pending_issues` only; serves a hard-coded scenario; one unit test wires through `linear.py` and confirms the call lands on the mock. Harness sets `OTEL_SDK_DISABLED=true`.
353	3. **`linear_mcp_shim` + first real-claude drain** — stdio MCP server implementing `save_issue` and `save_comment` against the mock; harness writes a `.mcp.json` registering it; first **real-claude** happy-path drain of one issue reaches Done end-to-end. Schemas captured from the official Linear MCP. Marked `@pytest.mark.real_claude`.
354	4. **`IntegrationHarness` fixture + subprocess smoke** — tmpdir HOME, repos.yml, real git repo, `.mcp.json` writer, mock startup. Slice 3's scenario refactored onto the harness. **Plus**: one subprocess test spawns `drain-cycle` against the mock end-to-end and asserts exit code plus runlog file existence.
355	5. **Mock GraphQL completeness + drift guard** — add `get_issue`, `set_state`, WorkflowState resolution; happy-path drain of 3 ordered issues passes. **Plus**: `test_linear_surface_audit` fails when any `_post(operation=...)` call site in `linear.py` lacks a mock handler.
356	6. **Fault injection** — per-operation, per-call-index fault modes in the mock; scenario covers `set_state` HTTP 500 halt (pre-spawn, fast). Per-scenario contract enforced (runlog assertions, mock state, call sequence).
357	7. **Pre-spawn halt scenarios** — repo-resolution failures, worktree-add failure, set_state 500. Fast, no claude.
358	8. **Real-claude halt scenarios** — child exits without Done (via `--max-turns 1`); child non-zero exit (via invalid prompt). Marked `real_claude`.
359	9. **Breach scenarios** — time-breach first (tight `per_issue_seconds` plus "wait 60s" prompt — robust). Token-breach gated on the slice-8 reproducibility spike. Both `real_claude`.
360	10. **Cycle-level halt scenarios** — `DependencyCycleError` (no claude); cycle-cap breach after two Done issues (`real_claude`).
361	11. **Resume scenario** — drive a halt, re-invoke orchestrator on the same mock cycle, assert second runlog file and final state. `real_claude`.
362	12. **Usage-tracking + Linear edge-case scenarios** — usage predicates on a happy-path drain (`real_claude`); 429 / malformed / slow-timeout halts on the parent (no claude).
363	
364	Slices 1–4 are the foundation; 5–12 are independent and ship in any order once 4 lands. Each slice opens a Linear issue under the initiative and closes per `AGENTS.md` (review → fix → commit + push → Linear summary comment → Done).

374	- `tests/integration/conftest.py` — harness fixture, `real_claude` marker config
375	- `tests/integration/mock_linear.py` — FastAPI GraphQL server + shared in-memory store + fault injection
376	- `tests/integration/linear_mcp_shim.py` — stdio MCP server backed by the shared store
377	- `tests/integration/prompts/*.md` — issue-body prompts (happy_path, exit_without_done, verbose, wait, etc.)
378	- `tests/integration/test_happy_path.py`, `test_pre_spawn_halts.py`, `test_real_claude_halts.py`, `test_breach.py`, `test_cycle_halts.py`, `test_resume.py`, `test_usage_tracking.py`, `test_linear_edges.py`, `test_linear_surface_audit.py`, `test_subprocess_smoke.py` — scenarios
379	
380	To reuse from existing tests:
381	- Real git-repo setup pattern in `tests/test_orchestrator_iteration.py:22-90`
382	- Linear fixture style in `tests/test_linear_pending_issues.py:65-70`
383	- `_CLAUDE_CMD` patch point at `drain_cycle/orchestrator.py:25`
384	
385	## Verification
386	
387	End-to-end:
388	- `uv run pytest tests/integration/ -v` — non-`real_claude` scenarios green; no real Linear network calls; no real `LINEAR_API_KEY` leaking in. The default local invocation.
389	- `uv run pytest tests/integration/ -m real_claude -v` — full suite green against a real Anthropic API key. Run on demand; instructions live in `tests/integration/README.md`.
390	- `uv run pytest --cov=drain_cycle/orchestrator --cov=drain_cycle/worker --cov=drain_cycle/runlog tests/integration/` — coverage on orchestrator, worker, and runlog rises meaningfully.
391	- Each slice closes per `AGENTS.md`: review → fix → commit + push to main → Linear summary comment → Done.
392	
393	**Halt-coverage assertion** (enforces KR 2):
394	- `test_halt_coverage` greps `mark_error` slugs from `drain_cycle/**.py`, collects the union of `exception.slug` attributes (or `halt_reason` strings) observed across the full integration run (including `real_claude`), and asserts equality. Fails locally when a new halt slug lands in production code without a covering scenario.
395	
396	Negative checks during build:
397	- `grep -r "api.linear.app" tests/` returns nothing — no accidental real-Linear calls in fixtures.
398	- The mock's call log (parent GraphQL + child MCP) records every operation a scenario expects; an unexpected call fails the test loudly.
399	- `test_linear_surface_audit` fails when any `_post(operation=...)` call site in `linear.py` lacks a mock handler.
400	- Cost budget guard: the `real_claude` suite prints total Anthropic spend at the end of each run; manual review flags runs over $5 (catch a runaway loop scenario early).
401

# Integration test architecture

**Linear initiative:** [Test drain-cycle failure modes and resume without a real Linear cycle](https://linear.app/ababushkin/project/test-drain-cycle-failure-modes-and-resume-without-a-real-linear-cycle-910b954af13f)

The OKR (Goal + Key Results + appetite + kill condition) is on the Linear project. This doc is the design — what gets built, why, in what order — and is the input to `shape-planning-and-task-breakdown` when the initiative is sliced into issues.

## Context

Today `drain-cycle` only tests against a real Linear cycle. Anything past happy-path becomes expensive: failure modes need deliberately broken issues, resume needs an actual halt-and-restart, and usage tracking needs real Claude sessions. Unit tests cover individual modules but stop at the function boundary — they monkey-patch `linear._post` and swap `claude -p` for marker-touching shell scripts (`tests/test_orchestrator_iteration.py:22`, `tests/test_linear_pending_issues.py:65`).

We need **scenario-level integration tests** that drive the orchestrator end-to-end against a controllable Linear surface, covering:

- **Best-case drains** — dependency-ordered execution, runlog shape, state transitions
- **Per-issue failure paths** — every halt condition in `orchestrator._drain_one_issue` (repo resolution, worktree setup, `set_state` failure, breach kill, not-Done exit)
- **Cycle-level halts** — `DependencyCycleError`, cycle-cap breach propagation
- **Resume semantics** — halt mid-cycle, re-run, observe pickup of in-flight issues reverted to Todo
- **Session/usage tracking** — `_UsageAccumulator` totals, cycle aggregates in runlog, per-issue caps triggering breaches

The drain has no native retry (deliberate, per `docs/design-decisions.md`); "retry" here means **resume** — a re-invocation against the same cycle.

## System context

The seam choice in one picture: real `claude -p`, mocked Linear, glued by an MCP shim that translates child MCP calls into GraphQL against the same in-memory store the parent hits over HTTP.

```svg
<svg viewBox="0 0 860 360" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Integration test system context: real harness, orchestrator, and claude-p on the left; mocked MockLinear, linear_mcp_shim, and shared in-memory store on the right.">
  <defs>
    <marker id="arr" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M0,0 L10,5 L0,10z" fill="currentColor"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="arr-accent" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M0,0 L10,5 L0,10z" fill="var(--accent)"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <rect x="300" y="8" width="540" height="344" rx="14" fill="none" stroke="var(--muted)" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="6 5"/>
  <text x="820" y="26" text-anchor="end" font="700 11px 'Inter', ui-sans-serif, sans-serif" fill="var(--muted)" letter-spacing="0.08em">MOCKED</text>

  <g class="p-node">
    <rect x="40" y="30" width="200" height="60" rx="10"/>
    <text x="140" y="56" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-title">IntegrationHarness</text>
    <text x="140" y="74" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-sub">pytest fixture</text>
  </g>
  <g class="p-node">
    <rect x="40" y="150" width="200" height="60" rx="10"/>
    <text x="140" y="176" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-title">orchestrator.run()</text>
    <text x="140" y="194" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-sub">parent · real</text>
  </g>
  <g class="p-node">
    <rect x="40" y="270" width="200" height="60" rx="10"/>
    <text x="140" y="296" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-title">claude -p</text>
    <text x="140" y="314" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-sub">child · real · per issue</text>
  </g>

  <g class="p-node">
    <rect x="340" y="30" width="200" height="60" rx="10"/>

    <text x="440" y="74" text-anchor="middle" class="p-node-sub">FastAPI · GraphQL</text>
  </g>
  <g class="p-node">
    <rect x="340" y="270" width="200" height="60" rx="10"/>
```
